B1.emotional_beat: A tight, irritated unease as Evan Kline sounds rehearsed—then the ‘EXEC-0441’ slip at 22:39 makes it feel like he’s been thinking about that exact record all day.

detective_annotations.content: ‘EXEC-0441’ + ‘22:39’ volunteered BEFORE exhibits shown — why does Kline know the label?
detective_annotations.style_hint: digital yellow highlight with a crisp callout

detective_annotations.content: He anchors to 20:03 call → ‘straight home.’ But keeps circling back to access policy. Cross-check B5 for 22:39 event details.
detective_annotations.style_hint: sans-serif note, slightly compressed like typed-in haste

detective_annotations.content: (Maybe wrong) If EXEC-0441 hit is real access, was Kline physically in Bldg 7 at 22:39? Don’t assume—verify.
detective_annotations.style_hint: small blue question icon + short note

B1.reading_order_note: Pairs best BEFORE B5: the impact is feeling sure Kline lied—then letting B5 complicate what ‘EXEC-0441’ actually proves.

B2.emotional_beat: A tug of empathy that curdles into suspicion when Nadia Reyes insists ‘badge-only’ at 21:55—then admits executive escort piggybacking makes the whole building timeline slippery.

detective_annotations.content: ‘Left at 21:55’ — lock this. Compare against any movement after 22:39 in B5.
detective_annotations.style_hint: thin red digital circle

detective_annotations.content: Key admission: ‘unless someone’s using executive escort… people slip through.’ That’s how you get bodies without badge hits.
detective_annotations.style_hint: grey callout box, understated

B2.reading_order_note: Read after B1 so ‘executive escort’ feels like a second witness independently describing the same loophole.

B3.emotional_beat: A creeping dread as Tom Briggs casually explains that ‘no page’ can be manufactured with a MAINT/SILENCED state—turning A1’s reassuring ‘no alarms’ line into something weaponizable.

detective_annotations.content: BRIGGS: ‘If it’s in maintenance, it won’t page me.’ This makes A1 ‘no alarm pages’ meaningless.
detective_annotations.style_hint: digital neon highlight + bolded excerpt

detective_annotations.content: Connect: ‘maintenance/silenced’ (B3) → verify actual state in system logs later (C2).
detective_annotations.style_hint: straight blue arrow with small label

detective_annotations.content: Who can set Stairwell 4B monitor to MAINT? Facilities only—or does QA/EHS admin have access too?
detective_annotations.style_hint: compact question badge

B3.reading_order_note: Works best right before B8 so the player recognizes ‘MAINT’ as a loaded word when it appears on the story capture.

B4.emotional_beat: A cold, controlled tension: Raina Patel’s 19:05 alibi sounds immaculate, but her fixation on ‘audit trails’ and ‘exports’ makes it feel like she’s negotiating evidence, not telling truth.

detective_annotations.content: Patel locks in: ‘Left at 19:05.’ Circle this—anything timestamped after-hours breaks her clean story.
detective_annotations.style_hint: pale yellow highlight + small clock icon

detective_annotations.content: She keeps steering to ‘authenticated session’ + ‘export’ language. Feels preemptive. Compare with B7 (review email) + later system export evidence.
detective_annotations.style_hint: tight sans-serif note in a light-blue callout

B4.reading_order_note: Read after B7 if possible; Patel’s evasions land harder once the DEV-102-17 request is fresh in mind.

B5.emotional_beat: An ‘oh no’ realization: the boring Lenel export doesn’t name a killer—it proves the building can be navigated on shared credentials and escort exceptions, meaning every confident alibi suddenly needs rechecking.

detective_annotations.content: 22:39:03 ACCESS GRANTED — CRED: EXEC-0441 (shared). This resolves CONTRA-01 but does NOT equal Kline.
detective_annotations.style_hint: red circle + bolded time

detective_annotations.content: ‘ESCORT MODE ENABLED’ + Host badge: QA-ADMIN-7719 (15 min). This matches Reyes’s ‘people slip through’ (B2).
detective_annotations.style_hint: digital yellow highlight with a pinned callout

detective_annotations.content: Look for any reader/location strings near Stairwell 4B / L4 corridor. Tie to A4 route + B6 cell sector STW4B.
detective_annotations.style_hint: small grey note like an analyst sidebar

B5.reading_order_note: This doc should land after B1 + B2 so it feels like a forensic ‘reality check’ on both interviews’ claims.

B6.emotional_beat: A tightening, claustrophobic urgency as Ellison’s last outgoing SMS at 23:11:52 (Sector: STW4B) is followed by a wall of missed calls—like a signal cutting off mid-sentence.

detective_annotations.content: 23:11:52 OUT — ‘Heading to the garage’ — Cell: HLX-B7-FEMTO-L4 (Sector: STW4B). This is minutes before 23:12.
detective_annotations.style_hint: bright highlight + digital sticky marker

detective_annotations.content: 22:06 SMS w/ ‘R. Patel’ re: ‘deviation folder’ → motive pressure. Pair with B7 DEV-102-17 language.
detective_annotations.style_hint: thin arrow with tiny label tag

detective_annotations.content: First ‘NO ANSWER/VM’ after 23:12 — treat as incapacitation window marker, not a random missed call.
detective_annotations.style_hint: compact note in a white callout box

B6.reading_order_note: Place before Envelope C: it primes the player to care about the 23:12 minute and recognize it when system logs appear.

B7.emotional_beat: A slow burn of anger and dread as Ellison demands raw audit-trail access (DEV-102-17 / Batch 14C) while Kline pushes to ‘take it off email’—and Patel offers only a curated ‘regulator-facing PDF package.’

detective_annotations.content: ELLISON 18:30 — ‘full DEV-102-17 folder’ + ‘raw audit-trail export’ + ‘before Monday 09:00.’ This is the fuse.
detective_annotations.style_hint: digital highlight with underlined deviation ID

detective_annotations.content: Kline after the 20:03 call: ‘off email’ pressure = fear of discoverability. Matches his tone in B1.
detective_annotations.style_hint: light-blue callout, clean sans-serif

detective_annotations.content: Patel offers ‘regulator-facing PDF package’ (curated). Compare to her 19:05 alibi claims in B4.
detective_annotations.style_hint: thin red circle + small ‘!’ in the margin

B7.reading_order_note: Read after B4 if possible: Patel’s interview becomes immediately suspect once you’ve seen how she frames ‘exports’ and ‘regulator-facing’ packages here.

B8.emotional_beat: A prickling sense of being watched as a jokey Instagram story places someone at Safety Panel SP-4B at 22:50 with a visible ‘MAINT’ banner—then the post vanishes by morning.

detective_annotations.content: METADATA: 2026-03-20 22:50 + label ‘SP-4B’ + banner ‘MAINT.’ That’s inside the critical period.
detective_annotations.style_hint: digital highlighter with a small pin icon

detective_annotations.content: Overlay: ‘night audit — don’t page Facilities’ → sounds like the exact ‘maintenance/silenced’ trick Briggs described in B3. Who posted, and why delete by ~07:30?
detective_annotations.style_hint: compact question callout with muted purple border

B8.reading_order_note: Best after B3 so ‘MAINT’ reads as a functional system state, not just a vague caption.
